content.content: (mcp__vvx__jjx completed with no output)

130	
131	## Capture-tool posture (crane embrace) — locked
132	
133	crane is the **sole image/registry tool** across every capture kind; skopeo, docker, and buildx are evicted from the capture path (separate eviction paces, bole first). The irreducible floor is three non-overlapping tools: **crane** (every registry/image op — `cp` by digest, `tag`, `append` to wrap an opaque blob), **curl** (non-registry HTTPS fetch — OAuth token, vendor rootfs tarball, the blob-residency HEAD guard), and **gpg** (vendor signature verify). All three are daemonless static binaries — the consolidation also removes the docker daemon from the capture path.
134	
135	- **oras** is documented equal-fidelity fallback only, not wired. The one future that reopens it: signed provenance via the OCI **referrers** API (oras is its reference implementation) — a *named* fork, never a silent inheritance of the crane pick.
136	- **skopeo's rule-out rationale is corrected** (cerebro 2026-06): it fatals *loud* on empty-config OCI artifacts (`oci.empty.v1+json`), not the silent foreign-layer skip first assumed — the disk blobs are `application/zstd`, distributable.
137	- **crane auth — resolved: gcrane's ambient Google keychain.** Plain `crane` has no per-command creds and reads only the docker-config keychain; the registry steps instead invoke **`gcrane`** (crane's Google-auth sibling, identical `cp`/`manifest`/`tag` engine) from `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`, whose `google.Keychain` matches `*.pkg.dev` and draws ADC → GCE-metadata-server credentials — so it auths GAR ambiently as the Mason SA with **no `crane auth login`, no credential-helper image, no in-memory token-fetch, no new IAM/repo grant**. This generalizes conclave's existing ambient docker auth (`gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`) to crane; the durable canon lands in RBSCB. (curl still mints a metadata token for the non-registry GAR blob-residency HEAD guard — curl's floor role, not gcrane's.)
138	- **skopeo eviction is total, including the cohort.** The made-side bind mirror (`rbfd_mirror` / `rbgjm01`) is the last skopeo holdout — a bare `skopeo copy --all` that converts cleanly to `crane cp`. Converting it is a *narrow, deliberate exception* to the capture-only boundary: it touches one made-side tool invocation, NOT the made-image package retrofit (Ark/Hallmark/abjure stay deferred to the sibling heat). Once the mirror is on crane and every capture consumer is gone, skopeo is dropped from the reliquary cohort entirely — the terminal functional pace, just before the vocabulary scrub.
139	
140	## Carried forward / consequences
141	
142	- **caparison-w / garrison-w for the `wsl` kind.** The workload's `rbtww-main` is registered via `wsl --import` from a seed tarball; today that seed is created by `wsl --export` of admin's installed distro (GarrisonWsl). Under the `wsl` kind, that export path retires — the seed becomes a verified artifact pulled from GAR. The pre-existing revert pace in ₣A- targeting the WSL-stage DEV CACHE shortcut becomes obsolete and wants dropping or transferring when this heat commits to development.
143	- **Windows onboarding is stale and deferred.** The `rbhw*` Windows handbook tracks (Docker Desktop, context discipline) are stale and incomplete, and `wsl`/`podvm` consumption is deferred — so their onboarding has nothing live to teach. Lean: mark those tracks loudly NOT-available / deferred rather than delete, so the eventual `wsl`-kind consumption work rereads the host-side prose rather than reconstructing it. Onboarding follows *consumption*, not *acquisition*: the Lode acquisition unification does NOT unify the onboarding surface; substrate-kind onboarding lands in the host/Windows tracks, never in the Director build-lifecycle tracks.
144	- **`inscribe` is verb-overloaded — disambiguate, don't conflate.** `rbfl_inscribe` (reliquary tool-mirror, `rbw-dI`) is **in scope** — the operation absorbed into the `reliquary` kind's capture verb (`conclave`). `rbrd_inscribe` (depot tripwire, `rbw-rdi`) is a **different operation, out of scope, stays put**. There is no `mirror` verb to retire: `rbfd_mirror` is the bind vessel-mode copy (made-side, also out of scope). What is absorbed is `inscribe`/reliquary, not `mirror`.
145	- **Onboarding rename surface (tri-surfaced vs greenfield).** `bole`/`reliquary` are fully present across bash, adoc, and the Director onboarding tracks (`rbw-Odf`/`Oda`/`Odb`) — for these the cost is *rename* (only two typed linked terms, `RBYC_ENSHRINE` + `RBYC_RELIQUARY`, drive the typed references; the rest is hand-edited prose). `wsl`/`podvm-*` are surfaced *nowhere* today — for these the cost is *greenfield*, not rename. Do not estimate the onboarding work as uniform.
146	- **Refresh cadence** is per-kind and may diverge (podvm-wsl vs podvm-native).
147	- **Maintenance verbs stay in the `image` family (`rbw-i*`), deliberately.** `rekon` / `audit` / `wrest` / `jettison` survive intact and do **not** move into `rbw-l*`. They are a low-level backdoor — primitives for cleaning up bad things at the member/tag grain — and that's where they belong; the `l` family is for the first-class Lode lifecycle (capture, the read-only `divine` enumeration and `augur` inspection, and `banish`). The split from the maintenance verbs is **grain, not redundancy**: `divine` enumerates **Lodes** by touchmark and `augur` inspects **one Lode** (both lifecycle-grain, `rbw-l*`); `audit`/`rekon` enumerate **members/tags within** a Lode (maintenance-grain, `rbw-i*`). Their fetched-side domain variants (Reliquary + Enshrinement) consolidate under Lode/touchmark addressing; the made-side Hallmark variants are out of scope, untouched.
148	- **Tabtarget shape is decided, not open:** per-kind capture verbs under the new `l` (Lode) command family — `rbw-lE/lC/lU/lI` capture, `rbw-ld` divine (read-only show), `rbw-lB` banish — superseding the earlier "unified `rbw-dE` with a kind argument" framing. (These move out of the Depot `d` family where `enshrine`/`inscribe`/`yoke` live today.)
149	- **Election (`yoke` + the deferred substrate verb) lives with its consumer, NOT in `l` — lowercase, and does not commit.** Election mutates the *consumer* (vessel for build-time kinds, nameplate for substrate kinds), not the Lode — the touchmark is its *argument*, the consumer its *target* — so it belongs in the consumer's family, paralleling made-side hallmark election (which lives with the nameplate/kludge machinery, never with ark/hallmark). `l` stays reserved for GAR create/destroy of the Lode itself. Three axes separate election from `l`-capture: it targets the **consumer** (not the Lode); it is **lowercase** (cheap reversible config, not persistent GAR/cost state); and it is **operator-committed** — it writes the config and emits a "commit with your usual workflow" hint, never self-committing. That last is the near-universal RBK convention: `yoke` today is explicit — RBSDY, *"this primitive does not commit; the caller commits"* — and the only RBK bash command that self-commits is Marshal-Zero (`rblm`), a wholesale reset ceremony that still gates a clean tree first. Election also does **not self-gate** on a dirty tree; the clean-tree guard lives at the downstream consumer (the build reading the vessel, charge reading the nameplate — `rbob_bottle` already gates the nameplate clean). Pace-time: `yoke` moves out of Depot `d` and de-capitalizes (`rbw-dY` → a lowercase consumer-family colophon); exact destination letter is a layout choice. (Capture verbs raise no git question at all — they write GAR, not git-tracked config.)

## Capture-tool posture (crane embrace) — locked

crane is the **sole image/registry tool** across every capture kind; skopeo, docker, and buildx are evicted from the capture path (separate eviction paces, bole first). The irreducible floor is three non-overlapping tools: **crane** (every registry/image op — `cp` by digest, `tag`, `append` to wrap an opaque blob), **curl** (non-registry HTTPS fetch — OAuth token, vendor rootfs tarball, the blob-residency HEAD guard), and **gpg** (vendor signature verify). All three are daemonless static binaries — the consolidation also removes the docker daemon from the capture path.

- **oras** is documented equal-fidelity fallback only, not wired. The one future that reopens it: signed provenance via the OCI **referrers** API (oras is its reference implementation) — a *named* fork, never a silent inheritance of the crane pick.
- **skopeo's rule-out rationale is corrected** (cerebro 2026-06): it fatals *loud* on empty-config OCI artifacts (`oci.empty.v1+json`), not the silent foreign-layer skip first assumed — the disk blobs are `application/zstd`, distributable.
- **crane auth — resolved: gcrane's ambient Google keychain.** Plain `crane` has no per-command creds and reads only the docker-config keychain; the registry steps instead invoke **`gcrane`** (crane's Google-auth sibling, identical `cp`/`manifest`/`tag` engine) from `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`, whose `google.Keychain` matches `*.pkg.dev` and draws ADC → GCE-metadata-server credentials — so it auths GAR ambiently as the Mason SA with **no `crane auth login`, no credential-helper image, no in-memory token-fetch, no new IAM/repo grant**. This generalizes conclave's existing ambient docker auth (`gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`) to crane; the durable canon lands in RBSCB. (curl still mints a metadata token for the non-registry GAR blob-residency HEAD guard — curl's floor role, not gcrane's.)
- **skopeo eviction is total, including the cohort.** The made-side bind mirror (`rbfd_mirror` / `rbgjm01`) is the last skopeo holdout — a bare `skopeo copy --all` that converts cleanly to `crane cp`. Converting it is a *narrow, deliberate exception* to the capture-only boundary: it touches one made-side tool invocation, NOT the made-image package retrofit (Ark/Hallmark/abjure stay deferred to the sibling heat). Once the mirror is on crane and every capture consumer is gone, skopeo is dropped from the reliquary cohort entirely — the terminal functional pace, just before the vocabulary scrub.

## Carried forward / consequences

Under the `wsl` kind, that export path retires — the seed becomes a verified artifact pulled from GAR. The pre-existing revert pace in ₣A- targeting the WSL-stage DEV CACHE shortcut becomes obsolete and wants dropping or transferring when this heat commits to development.
- **Windows onboarding is stale and deferred.** The `rbhw*` Windows handbook tracks (Docker Desktop, context discipline) are stale and incomplete, and `wsl`/`podvm` consumption is deferred — so their onboarding has nothing live to teach. Lean: mark those tracks loudly NOT-available / deferred rather than delete, so the eventual `wsl`-kind consumption work rereads the host-side prose rather than reconstructing it. Onboarding follows *consumption*, not *acquisition*: the Lode acquisition unification does NOT unify the onboarding surface; substrate-kind onboarding lands in the host/Windows tracks, never in the Director build-lifecycle tracks.
- **`inscribe` is verb-overloaded — disambiguate, don't conflate.** `rbfl_inscribe` (reliquary tool-mirror, `rbw-dI`) is **in scope** — the operation absorbed into the `reliquary` kind's capture verb (`conclave`). `rbrd_inscribe` (depot tripwire, `rbw-rdi`) is a **different operation, out of scope, stays put**. There is no `mirror` verb to retire: `rbfd_mirror` is the bind vessel-mode copy (made-side, also out of scope). What is absorbed is `inscribe`/reliquary, not `mirror`.
- **Onboarding rename surface (tri-surfaced vs greenfield).** `bole`/`reliquary` are fully present across bash, adoc, and the Director onboarding tracks (`rbw-Odf`/`Oda`/`Odb`) — for these the cost is *rename* (only two typed linked terms, `RBYC_ENSHRINE` + `RBYC_RELIQUARY`, drive the typed references; the rest is hand-edited prose). `wsl`/`podvm-*` are surfaced *nowhere* today — for these the cost is *greenfield*, not rename. Do not estimate the onboarding work as uniform.
- **Refresh cadence** is per-kind and may diverge (podvm-wsl vs podvm-native).
- **Maintenance verbs stay in the `image` family (`rbw-i*`), deliberately.** `rekon` / `audit` / `wrest` / `jettison` survive intact and do **not** move into `rbw-l*`. They are a low-level backdoor — primitives for cleaning up bad things at the member/tag grain — and that's where they belong; the `l` family is for the first-class Lode lifecycle (capture, the read-only `divine` enumeration and `augur` inspection, and `banish`). Their fetched-side domain variants (Reliquary + Enshrinement) consolidate under Lode/touchmark addressing; the made-side Hallmark variants are out of scope, untouched.

Three axes separate election from `l`-capture: it targets the **consumer** (not the Lode); it is **lowercase** (cheap reversible config, not persistent GAR/cost state); and it is **operator-committed** — it writes the config and emits a "commit with your usual workflow" hint, never self-committing. content.text:
All four threads are done and verified. Summary:

**1. Fragility worry — answered (above, sourced).** Bottom line: RB is built on Artifact Registry (the successor), not on the deprecated Container Registry storage product. The only gcr.io touch is bootstrap builders during reliquary *generation* — Google-owned images explicitly exempt from the shutdown, and non-load-bearing because sealed builds pull from our own GAR. Your pinning rule is the codebase's own existing invariant, not new risk.

**2. ₢BHAAT reslated** (committed). Re-scoped from "evict onto a floating gcrane" to four fronts under your pinning rule: Front A (docker→bootstrap gcrane in conclave, generation-tier), **Front B-prereq** (gcrane joins the reliquary cohort — the pinning floor), Front B (buildx→gcrane append landing on the *pinned* member), gate unchanged. The bootstrap-pin/our-AR-mirror is logged as a sibling itch, not this pace.

**3. Paddock principle recorded** (commit `fa40e145`, scoped to the paddock file only). The generation-vs-consumption pinning boundary now sits as a locked bullet in the crane-embrace posture section.

**4. RBSCB consolidation** — three edits, sitting as **uncommitted working-tree changes** for you to commit on your own workflow (tools never commit your specs):
- *Current Posture* → **Supply-chain pinning boundary** as the governing rule (generation may pull unpinned; sealed-reliquary builds carry zero unpinned aspects; durable home is our AR; capture brought into conformance by gcrane-as-cohort-member).
- *Deferred Items* → **Bootstrap builder pinning + gcr.io provenance** recorded decision, including the gcr.io-shutdown finding (Google-owned images exempt, RB stores nothing at gcr.io) and the hardening sibling-itch.
- *References* → new **Registry transition** group with the three Google AR-transition docs.

Two notes for your judgment:

- **Placement:** I put the canon in **RBSCB** (the Cloud Build posture spec — where the ambient-auth canon and "dependency mirroring is universal" invariant already live), not in RBS0-SpecTop itself. If you want the pinning boundary elevated to a top-level **`rbsk_` Key Premise** in RBS0, that's a deliberate quoin-mint I'd do on your say-so rather than slip in.
- The RBSCB edits are **prose + one reference sub-group only** — no new anchors, attributes, or quoins — so they can't break xref resolution. Say the word if you'd like a render/diff check before you commit.

Want me to also mint the `rbsk_` Key Premise in RBS0, or leave the canon homed in RBSCB?

843	{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
844	regime variable declares the operator's minimum concurrency requirement.
845	See
846	{rbtgo_quota_build}
847	for the manual Console procedure to review build capacity settings.
848	
849	== Key Premises
850	
851	[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]
852	//axl_voices axk_premise
853	{rbsk_no_gcloud}::
854	{at_recipe_bottle}
855	scripts MUST NOT invoke the `gcloud` CLI in local bash scripts or transitively through helper scripts.
856	All local
857	{gcp_service}
858	interaction uses direct REST API calls via `curl` with JWT-authenticated bearer tokens.
859	+
860	**Exception — vouch provenance fetch**: The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verification step (conjure mode only) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` for SLSA provenance envelope retrieval.
861	This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
862	All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
863	The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
864	Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
865	+
866	In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, graft, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
867	+
868	Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
869	The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
870	+
871	NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
872	The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.
873	
874	[[rbsk_in_pool_preflight]]
875	//axl_voices axk_premise
876	{rbsk_in_pool_preflight}::
877	Every ordain-path Cloud Build job — conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, and enshrine — prepends a step 0 (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
878	Conjure additionally HEAD-checks the vessel's enshrined base anchors (slots 1-3 from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`); other modes pass empty locator substitutions.
879	+
880	Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools — gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo.
881	Alpine, the preflight step's own image, is validated implicitly by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs.
882	Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain).
883	+
884	Rationale: defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR).
885	The in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap private-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew.
886	+
887	Scope boundary: standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered.
888	
889	[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
890	//axl_voices axk_premise
891	{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
892	{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
893	The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
894	Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
895	Batched invest is not supported — by design.
896	+
897	Rationale: a singleton assay slot keeps the producer-output discipline uniform across roles and frees the implementation from path-space per-identity routing.
898	The operator-driven move step is the deliberate promotion gate from producer output to role-production consumer; it also acts as the operator's explicit acknowledgement of which identity owns the role at runtime, since `RBRA` files encode exactly one identity at a time.
899	Move (not copy) prevents the assay slot from accumulating stale credential bytes after promotion.
900	
901	== Dependency Inventory
902	
903	{at_recipe_bottle} depends on a minimal, explicitly managed set of external commands.
904	The Bash Console Guide (`BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md`) establishes the universal dependency discipline — POSIX builtins preferred, external commands justified individually.
905	This section inventories the concrete choices {at_recipe_bottle} has made within that framework.
906	
907	=== Consumer Dependencies
908	
909	Required on every {at_workstation}.
910	Enforced by qualification and documented on the project page.

file.content:
{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
regime variable declares the operator's minimum concurrency requirement.
See
{rbtgo_quota_build}
for the manual Console procedure to review build capacity settings.

== Key Premises

[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_no_gcloud}::
{at_recipe_bottle}
scripts MUST NOT invoke the `gcloud` CLI in local bash scripts or transitively through helper scripts.
All local
{gcp_service}
interaction uses direct REST API calls via `curl` with JWT-authenticated bearer tokens.
+
**Exception — vouch provenance fetch**: The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verification step (conjure mode only) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` for SLSA provenance envelope retrieval.
This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
+
In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, graft, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
+
Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
+
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.

[[rbsk_in_pool_preflight]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_in_pool_preflight}::
Every ordain-path Cloud Build job — conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, and enshrine — prepends a step 0 (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
Conjure additionally HEAD-checks the vessel's enshrined base anchors (slots 1-3 from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`); other modes pass empty locator substitutions.
+
Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools — gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo.
Alpine, the preflight step's own image, is validated implicitly by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs.
Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain).
+
Rationale: defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR).
The in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap private-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew.
+
Scope boundary: standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered.

[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
Batched invest is not supported — by design.
+
Rationale: a singleton assay slot keeps the producer-output discipline uniform across roles and frees the implementation from path-space per-identity routing.
The operator-driven move step is the deliberate promotion gate from producer output to role-production consumer; it also acts as the operator's explicit acknowledgement of which identity owns the role at runtime, since `RBRA` files encode exactly one identity at a time.
Move (not copy) prevents the assay slot from accumulating stale credential bytes after promotion.

== Dependency Inventory

{at_recipe_bottle} depends on a minimal, explicitly managed set of external commands.
The Bash Console Guide (`BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md`) establishes the universal dependency discipline — POSIX builtins preferred, external commands justified individually.
This section inventories the concrete choices {at_recipe_bottle} has made within that framework.

=== Consumer Dependencies

Required on every {at_workstation}.
Enforced by qualification and documented on the project page.

input.new_string:
Move (not copy) prevents the assay slot from accumulating stale credential bytes after promotion.

[[rbsk_pinning_boundary]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_pinning_boundary}::
A build that consumes a sealed reliquary MUST carry zero unpinned aspects: every tool image and base image it pulls resolves, content-pinned, from project-controlled {gar_service} (the reliquary cohort and the enshrined anchors), never from a mutable upstream tag.
Reliquary _generation_ — the inscribe/conclave ceremony that mints the sealed cohort — is the one and only phase permitted to pull unpinned upstream images, since the first tool must be bootstrapped from somewhere.
+
The boundary is staged on the reliquary itself: once a reliquary is sealed, everything downstream of it is pinned; everything that produces it may not yet be.
The capture family obeys the same boundary as the made side — the capture image tool (`gcrane`) is a reliquary cohort member, so a sealed-reliquary capture (bole/wsl/podvm) resolves a pinned `gcrane`; only conclave, which builds the reliquary and therefore cannot resolve from it, rides the floating Google-hosted bootstrap builder.
+
Rationale: a result that depends on whatever tag happened to be current at build time is a versioning failure, not a convenience — the same anti-ambient-state ethos as content-addressed anchors, the depot tripwire, and SLSA vouch.
The narrow bootstrap exception is bounded, supervised, and re-runnable; it touches nothing a consumer relies on.
The durable supply-chain canon, the gcr.io-bootstrap-only provenance, and the per-grade trust details live in RBSCB.

== Dependency Inventory

content.text:
Done — tree clean, premise minted and notched as `6fbd459c5` (heat ₣BH). `rbsk_pinning_boundary` is live in RBS0 (mapping + definition with the `axk_premise` voicing), and it bundled the companion RBSCB edits into the same canon commit.

Here's the resume prompt — paste into a clean chat:

```
mount BH — execute pace ₢BHAAT (crane-embrace-eviction). Planning is cinched;
this session implements. Read the paddock and the reslated docket first, confirm
the plan against current code, and propose execution order before touching code —
don't re-litigate the cinched decisions.

Three things you won't get from reading code alone, settled in the planning
session (2026-06-09):

- The gcrane-append spike CLEARED — verified live on cerebro that `gcrane append`
  with no base (`--oci-empty-base` for OCI media) builds + pushes a FROM-scratch
  single-layer image on gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug. Front B is
  unblocked; do NOT re-spike.

- The operator pinning rule is now governing canon (RBS0 Key Premise
  rbsk_pinning_boundary, RBSCB "Supply-chain pinning boundary", and the paddock):
  reliquary GENERATION may pull unpinned upstream; any build CONSUMING a sealed
  reliquary carries zero unpinned aspects. Consequence: gcrane must become a
  reliquary cohort member so sealed captures resolve a PINNED gcrane; only
  conclave (which builds the reliquary) keeps the floating bootstrap
  ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER.

- gcr.io fragility was investigated and dismissed (RBSCB decision): RB stores
  everything in Artifact Registry; the gcr.io touch is bootstrap-generation-only
  and Google-owned-image-exempt from the Container Registry shutdown. Don't reopen.

Execution order from the reslated docket:
  1. Front B-prereq — gcrane joins the reliquary cohort (the pinning floor). Mint
     a RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_* member; verify zrbfc_resolve_tool_images carries it to
     the vouch builder substitution. Do this FIRST.
  2. Front A — docker→gcrane in conclave (generation-tier; bootstrap gcrane OK).
  3. Front B — buildx→gcrane append (rbgjl02 + underpin), consumption rows landing
     on the PINNED reliquary gcrane. Watch the underpin curl/gpg-on-busybox wrinkle
     and the rbgjv03 shared-snippet boundary (both flagged in the docket).
  4. Gate — service-tier lode + reliquary + wsl lifecycle fixtures green against
     live GAR, run once.

Fronts A and B are file-disjoint — fan to parallel sub-agents once B-prereq lands.
```

One honest caveat to carry over: the fresh session should re-verify the `zrbfc_resolve_tool_images → builder-substitution` wiring before committing Front B-prereq — I traced that bole's vouch resolves tools from the reliquary, but I didn't open the resolver itself. That verification is the first real step of B-prereq, and the docket says so.
